ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-14-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE CITY HAS SATISFIED ITS BURDEN THAT PLAINTIFF BEVERLY HYBL CANNOT PROVE EITHER OF HER TWO CLAIMS AGAINST THE CITY AND MS. HYBL HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE DISPUTE AS TO EITHER OF THOSE CLAIMS. MS. HYBL'S OBJECTIONS TO THE DECLARATIONS OF BOB ROTISKI AND TERESA RIVOR ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-14-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rosaline Ayoub of Quintairos, Prieto, Wood & Boyer, P.A. telephone number 213-486-0048 appeared by telephone for Defendant Prime Flight Aviation Services.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is granted. The City has satisfied its burden that plaintiff Beverly Hybl cannot prove either of her two claims against the City and Ms. Hybl has failed to present any evidence creating a triable dispute as to either of those claims. As to the third cause of action for general negligence, the City has shown that it did not provide wheelchair transport service to Ms. Hybl on the day she fell nor did the City instruct Ms. Hybl regarding her use of the passenger boarding bridge where Ms. Hybl fell. (City's Facts 1-5). Based on these facts, the City did not owe any duty to Ms. Hybl regarding the wheelchair transport service or instructions for the use of the passenger boarding bridge. As to the fourth cause of action for premises liability, the City has shown that on the day Ms. Hybl fell the City did not own, maintain, manage or operate the passenger boarding bridge where Ms. Hybl fell. (City's Fact 6). Based on these facts, the City did not owe any duty to Ms. Hybl regarding the passenger boarding bridge. Ms. Hybl's objections to the declarations of Bob Rotiski and Teresa Rivor are overruled. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
